Ammunition Types and PEN Modifiers.Description: Standard round designed for general-purpose use, offers reliable performance without specialized characteristics.
Designed for maximum tissue damage, but loses penetration ability due to expansion on impact.
Specialized round designed to penetrate body armor and hard targets, increasing penetration capability.
Designed to expand upon impact, providing a balance between tissue damage and penetration.
Round that leaves a visible trail, used to help with targeting, but slightly reduces penetration due to the added components.
Explosive rounds that prioritize damage over penetration, often used for area suppression or anti-materiel purposes.
Ammunition designed with higher velocity to improve penetration at long ranges.
Round designed to ignite upon impact, often used for causing fires or disabling equipment, but reduces penetration.
Enhanced FMJ with a boat-tail design, offering increased accuracy and range while slightly improving penetration.
Lower velocity rounds designed for quiet shooting, resulting in reduced penetration but quieter operation.
Armor-piercing round with incendiary capabilities, offering increased penetration and fire-starting ability.
Combination of armor-piercing and explosive effects, designed to penetrate and then cause internal explosions.
Single projectile used in shotguns, capable of greater penetration compared to traditional shotguns pellets.
Small dart-like projectiles used for greater spread and penetration of soft targets.
Highly specialized round designed for heavy armor penetration, often used against tanks and armored vehicles.
Shotgun shells that expel fire upon firing, with extreme reduction in penetration but effective for creating fire hazards.
Designed for explosive damage at impact, sacrificing penetration ability for explosive effects.
Ammunition with a hardened steel core designed to increase penetration, especially against soft armor.
Ammunition with a tungsten core designed for extreme armor-piercing capability, often used in military applications.
